title: AI Scam & Fraud Tracker
description: AI-enabled scam and fraud types with government advisories, documented incidents, loss statistics, and primary sources from FBI IC3, FTC, FinCEN, CISA, FCC, SEC, and DOJ. Not legal or security advice; confirm against the linked primary sources.
citation: Vorp Labs, AI Scam & Fraud Tracker (as of 2026-07-10), https://vorplabs.com/ai-threat-monitoring

scamTypes.name: Voice-cloning family-emergency scams
scamTypes.mechanism: A short voice sample is used to synthesize a relative’s voice. The caller claims a crash, arrest, kidnapping, or medical emergency and demands irreversible payment via wire, cryptocurrency, gift cards, or courier.

advisories.title: Scammers use AI to enhance their family emergency schemes
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
Criminals Using Altered Proof-of-Life Media to Extort Victims in Virtual Kidnapping for Ransom Scams

incidents.label: IC3 2025: victims reported over $5 million in losses to AI-enabled distress scams

factChecks.label: FBI PSA
factChecks.value: FBI IC3 I-120324-PSA documents voice cloning for family-emergency financial fraud schemes.

factChecks.label: FTC advisory
factChecks.value: FTC published a consumer alert on 2023-03-20 warning that scammers use AI-cloned voices in family emergency schemes.

openItems: DOJ grandparent-scam prosecutions (DNJ, SDCA, DMA, DRI) establish the underlying scheme, but none of the available press releases confirm AI voice cloning was used.

scamTypes.name: Deepfake executive impersonation and business email compromise
scamTypes.mechanism: Voice-cloned or deepfake video avatars of executives, CFOs, vendors, or lawyers are used to direct employees to transfer funds, change invoices, or disclose credentials. AI is combined with compromised email, messaging apps, and fake virtual meetings.

advisories.title: Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
FinCEN Alert on Fraud Schemes Involving Deepfake Media Targeting Financial Institutions

incidents.label: IC3 2025: businesses reported over $30 million in losses to BEC scams involving AI

factChecks.label: IC3 AI BEC losses
factChecks.value: IC3 2025 Annual Report states businesses reported losses over $30 million to BEC scams involving AI.

factChecks.label: FinCEN deepfake alert
factChecks.value: FinCEN FIN-2024-Alert004 describes deepfake media targeting financial institutions, including AI-altered identity documents and real-time deepfake video calls.

openItems: No U.S. federal enforcement action is tied to a specific deepfake BEC incident as of this update. The Arup Hong Kong case ($25.6 million) is confirmed by the company but has no U.S. government primary source.

scamTypes.name: AI-enhanced romance and pig-butchering investment scams
scamTypes.mechanism: AI-generated personas, images, scripts, translations, and chatbots scale romance and trust-based investment schemes and crypto-investment fraud. Victims are moved to private messaging apps and shown fake trading profits on sham platforms.

advisories.title: Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
Fraudsters Target US Stock Investors through Investment Clubs Accessed on Social Media and Messaging Applications
FinCEN Alert on Prevalent Virtual Currency Investment Scam Commonly Known as ‘Pig Butchering’

incidents.label: IC3 2025: romance and trust-based scams with reported AI nexus caused over $19 million in losses
IC3 2025: cryptocurrency investment fraud caused $7.2 billion in reported losses (not all AI-enabled)

relatedEnforcement.label: SEC v. Delphia / Global Predictions (AI washing)
SEC v. Rimar Capital (AI automated trading claims)

factChecks.label: IC3 AI romance losses
factChecks.value: IC3 2025 Annual Report AI section states romance and trust-based scams with reported AI nexus caused over $19 million in losses.

factChecks.label: FinCEN pig-butchering alert
factChecks.value: FinCEN FIN-2023-Alert005 (2023-09-08) describes virtual currency investment scam patterns commonly known as pig butchering.

openItems: DOJ pig-butchering prosecutions exist, but no available DOJ press release establishes that AI was used in those cases.
FinCEN FIN-2023-Alert005: the FinCEN advisories index confirms the alert ID, date, and title; the direct PDF URL has not been located.

scamTypes.name: Deepfake celebrity-endorsement investment scams
scamTypes.mechanism: AI-generated video, images, or voice of celebrities, CEOs, or public figures is used in social ads, investment-club funnels, or private chats to make fraudulent investment opportunities appear endorsed.

advisories.title: Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

incidents.label: IC3 2025: investment complaints with reported AI nexus surpassed $632 million

relatedEnforcement.label: SEC v. Delphia / Global Predictions (AI washing, 2024-03-18)
SEC v. Rimar Capital (AI automated trading, 2024-10-11)

factChecks.label: IC3 AI investment losses
factChecks.value: IC3 2025 Annual Report states investment complaints with reported AI nexus surpassed $632 million; overall investment scams exceeded $8 billion.

openItems: SEC Investor.gov published an OIEA Investor Alert on AI and investment fraud (confirmed in SEC press releases), but the exact Investor.gov URL has not been located.

scamTypes.name: Fake AI tools and deceptive AI services
scamTypes.mechanism: Operators sell AI-branded products that do not work as promised or generate deceptive content. Categories include an alleged “AI lawyer,” AI-branded money-making storefronts, AI fake-review generators, and AI telemarketing tools. Criminals also embed AI chatbots in fraudulent websites to automate victim engagement.

advisories.title: FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

incidents.label: FTC Operation AI Comply: actions against AI fake-review tool, alleged AI lawyer, and AI-branded money-making storefront schemes
FTC Air AI Technologies final order entered (case 2:25-cv-03068-SMB)

relatedEnforcement.label: FTC v. Ascend Ecom / Empire Holdings (AI storefronts)
FTC v. FBA Machine / Passive Scaling (AI storefronts)

factChecks.label: Operation AI Comply
factChecks.value: FTC announced Operation AI Comply on 2024-09-25 with actions involving Automators AI, Rytr, DoNotPay, Ascend Ecom, Empire Holdings, and FBA Machine/Passive Scaling.

factChecks.label: Air AI order
factChecks.value: FTC Air AI Technologies final order entered 2026-03-24 in D. Ariz. (case 2:25-cv-03068-SMB).

sources.label: FTC Operation AI Comply press release

scamTypes.name: AI-generated phishing and spear phishing at scale
scamTypes.mechanism: Generative AI lowers the cost of producing convincing targeted lures, social profiles, translations, spear-phishing content, and fake support flows. Attackers steal credentials, MFA codes, contact lists, or financial account access.

advisories.title: Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
Russian Intelligence Services Target Commercial Messaging Application Accounts
Cyber Criminals Target Victims Using Social Engineering Techniques

incidents.label: IC3 2025: phishing/spoofing generated 191,561 complaints and $215,843,126 in losses (not all AI-enabled)

factChecks.label: IC3 phishing category
factChecks.value: IC3 2025 Annual Report lists phishing/spoofing as 191,561 complaints and $215,843,126 in losses. This is the overall phishing category, not limited to AI-generated phishing.

factChecks.label: FBI/CISA messaging app advisory
factChecks.value: FBI/CISA I-032026-PSA (2026-03-20) warns of Russian intelligence campaign compromising commercial messaging app accounts through phishing.

openItems: No AI-phishing-specific federal prosecution with a dollar amount has been identified as of this update.

scamTypes.name: AI-generated sextortion, nonconsensual intimate deepfakes, and AI CSAM
scamTypes.mechanism: Criminals generate or alter sexual imagery using AI, then extort victims or distribute the material. The FBI states that AI-generated CSAM is illegal under federal law. The Take It Down Act created new criminal penalties for publishing nonconsensual intimate digital forgeries.

advisories.title: Child Sexual Abuse Material Created by Generative AI and Similar Online Tools is Illegal
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

incidents.label: IC3 2025: more than 75,000 sextortion submissions; more than 5,700 involving minors referred to NCMEC
DOJ S.D. Ohio: James Strahler II pleaded guilty to cyberstalking, producing obscene visual representations of child sexual abuse, and publication of digital forgeries using AI
DOJ E.D.N.Y.: Shannon and Hernandez arrested for publishing AI deepfake pornography in violation of the Take It Down Act

factChecks.label: Strahler guilty plea
factChecks.value: DOJ press release confirms James Strahler II pleaded guilty on 2026-04-07 in S.D. Ohio to cyberstalking and producing AI-generated obscene material of children.

factChecks.label: Shannon/Hernandez arrests
factChecks.value: DOJ press release confirms Shannon and Hernandez were arrested on 2026-05-20 on complaints alleging Take It Down Act violations for AI-generated deepfake pornography.

factChecks.label: Domain seizures
factChecks.value: DOJ seized domain names used for publishing nude digital forgeries of famous women.

scamTypes.name: AI voice phishing and IT-helpdesk social engineering
scamTypes.mechanism: Attackers impersonate employees or support staff, call help desks, and request MFA resets, SIM swaps, call forwarding, or credential resets. FBI IC3 PSA I-120324 notes AI voice can make vishing more credible. CISA/FBI advisory AA23-320A documents related Scattered Spider social-engineering and intrusion tactics but does not specifically address AI voice.

advisories.title: Cyber Criminals Target Victims Using Social Engineering Techniques
Scattered Spider
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

factChecks.label: IC3 AI employment scam losses
factChecks.value: IC3 2025 Annual Report states victims reported almost $13 million in AI-involved employment-type scams. This IC3 category includes fake job offers and deepfake interviews, not limited to helpdesk vishing.

factChecks.label: Scattered Spider advisory
factChecks.value: CISA/FBI Joint Cybersecurity Advisory AA23-320A documents Scattered Spider social engineering tactics including fake social profiles and joining incident-response calls. This advisory does not specifically address AI voice.

openItems: Federal indictments or court-loss amounts specific to Scattered Spider have not been identified as of this update.
North Korean IT-worker alerts referenced in IC3 appendices have not been individually linked here.

scamTypes.name: AI-generated fake reviews and deceptive listings
scamTypes.mechanism: AI writing tools generate fabricated testimonials and reviews at high volume. AI images create fake photos for products, rentals, or listings. The FTC finalized a rule banning fake reviews and testimonials, including those generated by AI.

advisories.title: FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

factChecks.label: FTC fake-review rule
factChecks.value: FTC AI accomplishments paper states FTC finalized a rule banning fake reviews and testimonials, including those generated by AI.

factChecks.label: Rytr FTC action
factChecks.value: FTC alleged Rytr’s review-generation service could produce false/deceptive review content at scale; part of Operation AI Comply.

openItems: No standalone federal advisory on AI-generated fake rental or real-estate listings has been identified as of this update.

sources.label: FTC Operation AI Comply press release

scamTypes.name: Impersonation of government officials via AI voice and text
scamTypes.mechanism: Malicious actors send texts and AI-generated voice messages purporting to be senior U.S. officials. They seek rapport, migration to encrypted messaging apps, credential theft, contact-list compromise, or funds.

advisories.title: Senior US Officials Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign
Senior U.S. Officials Continue to be Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign

incidents.label: FTC 2025 data: government impersonators caused about $920 million in losses (not all AI-enabled)

relatedEnforcement.label: FCC v. Lingo Telecom (AI robocall settlement)
FCC v. Steve Kramer (deepfake Biden robocalls)

factChecks.label: FBI PSA I-051525
factChecks.value: FBI IC3 I-051525-PSA (2025-05-15) states that since April 2025, malicious actors have impersonated senior U.S. officials using text and AI-generated voice messages.

factChecks.label: FTC imposter losses
factChecks.value: FTC data release (2026-06) states government impersonators caused about $920 million in losses in 2025. This is the overall government-impersonation category, not limited to AI.

openItems: FBI PSAs describe impersonation of senior U.S. officials but do not name specific individuals. Publicly reported incidents involving named officials require separate official confirmation before they can be included here.

scamTypes.name: Deepfake synthetic identity and KYC fraud
scamTypes.mechanism: Deepfake images, fake identity documents, synthetic media, and spoofed biometrics are used to bypass financial-institution identity verification and open or access accounts. FinCEN reports increased suspicious activity reporting describing suspected deepfake media.

advisories.title: FinCEN Alert on Fraud Schemes Involving Deepfake Media Targeting Financial Institutions
Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

factChecks.label: FinCEN SAR reporting
factChecks.value: FinCEN FIN-2024-Alert004 states it observed increased suspicious activity reporting describing suspected deepfake media, particularly fraudulent identity documents used to circumvent identity verification/authentication.

factChecks.label: SAR key term
factChecks.value: FinCEN provides SAR key term FIN-2024-DEEPFAKEFRAUD for financial institutions to flag suspected deepfake fraud in suspicious activity reports.

openItems: No federal prosecution tied specifically to the FinCEN deepfake-KYC typology has been identified as of this update.
FinCEN FIN-2024-Alert004 is a typology and SAR-filing alert, not a public loss-statistics report. It does not provide aggregate dollar-loss figures.

scamTypes.name: Election deepfake robocalls and public-figure voice cloning
scamTypes.mechanism: AI voice cloning creates a public figure’s voice and robocall networks distribute the message with spoofed caller ID to mislead voters or impersonate public officials.

advisories.title: FCC NAL FCC 24-59: Steve Kramer deepfake Biden robocalls
FCC cease-and-desist to Lingo Telecom

incidents.label: New Hampshire AI Biden robocalls: FCC found Steve Kramer caused thousands of illegal prerecorded voice robocalls using a deepfake AI voice imitating President Biden
FCC proposed $6,000,000 forfeiture against Steve Kramer
Lingo Telecom agreed to $1,000,000 civil penalty and KYC/KYUP compliance measures

relatedEnforcement.label: FCC v. Steve Kramer (deepfake Biden robocalls, FCC 24-59)
FCC v. Lingo Telecom (AI robocall settlement)

factChecks.label: FCC Kramer NAL
factChecks.value: FCC NAL FCC 24-59 (released 2024-05-24) proposes $6 million forfeiture for deepfake Biden robocalls with spoofed caller ID. File No. EB-TCD-24-00036094.

factChecks.label: Lingo settlement
factChecks.value: FCC release states Lingo Telecom agreed to $1 million civil penalty and KYC/KYUP compliance measures.

stats.value: About $16 billion; imposter scam losses $3.5 billion
stats.note: FTC figures are not AI-specific unless the FTC text explicitly says so. FTC and IC3 are separate reporting systems and should not be summed.

stats.value: More than 75,000 submissions; more than 5,700 involving minors referred to NCMEC
stats.sourceTitle: FBI IC3 2025 Annual Report, sextortion section
stats.note: Not all AI-specific. IC3 2025 is the first year with an AI-related descriptor.

changeLog.label: Page launched
changeLog.change: Published AI scam and fraud tracker covering scam types documented by FBI IC3, FTC, FinCEN, CISA, FCC, SEC, and DOJ, with government-reported statistics and cross-links to the federal AI enforcement tracker.

changeLog.label: FTC imposter scam data
changeLog.change: FTC released 2025 imposter scam data: $3.5 billion in losses, government impersonators about $920 million.

changeLog.label: Take It Down Act arrests
changeLog.change: DOJ E.D.N.Y. arrested Shannon and Hernandez on Take It Down Act complaints for AI-generated deepfake pornography.

changeLog.label: Strahler guilty plea
changeLog.change: James Strahler II pleaded guilty in S.D. Ohio to cyberstalking and producing AI-generated obscene material of children.

changeLog.label: FTC Air AI final order
changeLog.change: FTC Air AI Technologies final order entered in D. Ariz.

changeLog.label: FBI/CISA messaging app advisory
changeLog.change: FBI/CISA I-032026-PSA warned of Russian intelligence campaign using phishing to compromise commercial messaging app accounts.

monitoringSources.label: FBI IC3 public service announcements
monitoringSources.note: Primary source for AI-fraud PSAs. Annual reports published yearly.

monitoringSources.note: 2025 is the first report with an AI-related descriptor and loss category.

monitoringSources.note: Covers enforcement actions, data releases, and rulemaking.
